Notice Of Petition & Petition For An Order Granting Her Authority To Maintain And Continue The Survival Causes Of Action On Behalf Of Deberah Minkin, As Personal/Legal Representative; Declaration Of Jacob L. Stipp In Support
Off Calendar Dec-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Counsel for Plaintiff Mr. Stipp appears in an abundance of caution. The Court did not receive any notice to contest the court's tentative ruling.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Charlee Minkin's Petition for Order Granting Her Authority to Maintain and Continue the Survival Causes of Action on Behalf of Deberah Minkin as Personal and Legal Representative is granted.

Jane Doe, aka Deberah Minkin, died on November 5, 2025. The evidence establishes that Jane Doe appointed Charlee Minkin to serve as the successor trustee of the Restated and Amended Deb Minkin Trust, and the executor of her will. (Declaration of Charlee Minton, Exh. A) Ms. Minkin thus qualifies to serve as the decedent's personal representative for the purpose of pursuing the survival causes of action alleged in the complaint. (Probate Code 58; Code of Civil Procedure 377.31)

Efforts by defendants San Francisco Health Care and Rehab, Inc., et.al. ("Defendants") to muddy the waters by arguing numerous issues irrelevant to this motion, including, but not limited to, whether Plaintiff has established a likelihood of succeeding on the merits of her claims, are unhelpful. While Defendants correctly point out that Plaintiff's motion was served electronically with 16 days notice rather than the required 18 days notice, the court overrules this objection and elects to hear the merits of the motion. It is clear that Defendants suffered no prejudice from the shortened notice as they were able to file a detailed Opposition to the motion. Moreover, it would be a waste of court time and the parties' resources to continue the hearing on a clear-cut legal issue based on this de minimus procedural violation. The court further finds that Plaintiff's inadvertent failure to attach a copy of the decedent's will to the Declaration of Charlee Minkin in support of the motion does not warrant denial of the motion. Copies of the decedent's will and trust were attached initially to the declaration of Jay Stipp, and thus available for Defendant's review. The copies of the will and trust which were later authenticated by the amended Declaration of Charlee Minkin are identical to the copies attached to Stipp's declaration.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CM)
